title: No Arm Hair Random Noticing
wikitext:
[]: No Arm Hair? -- Random Noticing             Quote Post by Duke » Sat Aug 07, 2010 12:54 am So, I was watching episode 8, and being my weird self, I noticed that the navy people on the ship (...Americans? I don't know) seem to have a shit ton of arm hair, while none of the main characters do. are the Japanese just hair less? xD also, how the arm hair is visible from so far away. (The guy behind the Admiral) Oh btw, Kensuke is pretty creepy there...

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Aug 07, 2010 1:04 am All of the people you're comparing them to in the frame are some combination of the "middle school student" and "female" person categories. Edit: Oh, I had to scroll over to see Kaji. Well, you know. He's, uh.. he's Kaji. It's just one of those things, alright?

[]:             Quote Post by Voland » Sat Aug 07, 2010 1:28 am In general, asians tend to have nearly no hair on the arms, legs and chest. Japanese have the most out of all asians, probably from the amount of raw fish eaten (read: assumption, no scientific proof to back it up).

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sat Aug 07, 2010 4:44 am It is a benign racial stereotype of white hairy men.

[]:             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Sat Aug 07, 2010 6:22 pm For some reason when i saw your thread, i though it said No Arm chair, instead of hair lol. So what if some have hair and some don't- is it really missing? Besides they probably look better without hair

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Sat Aug 07, 2010 9:45 pm It just struck me as odd that the men on the ship are like gorillas when the rest of them are hairless.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sat Aug 07, 2010 9:49 pm Maybe they are trying to stereotype sailors as big men, that have a lot of hair?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Aug 07, 2010 11:24 pm struck me as odd that the men on the ship are like gorillas when the rest of them are hairless.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel » Sun Aug 08, 2010 1:59 am Picture Shinji with arm hair. Just try. It wouldn't work too well. I think it's better without the main characters having arm hair like that. It would be a little annoying. These sailors are pretty much never seen again and don't really do too much moving while they're on screen, so it's no skin off anyone's back to give them slightly silly features like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Sun Aug 08, 2010 2:08 am Ramiel wrote: Picture Shinji with arm hair. Just try. It wouldn't work too well.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Aug 08, 2010 12:52 pm The American characters are American stereotypes. Extends to body hair.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Aug 08, 2010 9:07 pm Marry me, Ramiel.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Aug 08, 2010 9:52 pm TehDonutKing wrote: Marry me, Ramiel.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Aug 08, 2010 10:38 pm ^not to defend such madness, but that kind of thing is legal in some jurisdictions believe it or not

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Aug 08, 2010 10:50 pm Typically, characters with arm hair are usually the ugly, the old, or the extremely masculine (but not always the case for each) or even when trying to meet stereotypes. It's one of those details used to make a character more appear a certain way, such as lines on the face, being overweight, and having acne; all of these are generally viewed as unattractive by many people. How many anime characters do you know with hairy legs? Just think "Typical Hollywood." Other examples of body hair include: Huang (Darker Than Black), Mr. Satan (DBZ), Lord Genome (TTGL)

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel » Sun Aug 08, 2010 11:09 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: ^not to defend such madness, but that kind of thing is legal in some jurisdictions believe it or not

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Mon Aug 09, 2010 2:03 am Ramiel, I'm your age. Therefore, my love of you is legal. until your internet filter banhammers me for being American

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Aug 09, 2010 2:06 am Joseph the PRPD wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Mon Aug 09, 2010 2:11 am TehDonutKing wrote: ....I'm 14.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Aug 09, 2010 7:52 am TOPIC.
